headlineSummary: Hacker News sentiment skewed negative for Google Gemini on privacy complaints, while OpenAI and Anthropic drew mixed reactions that included criticism of business focus alongside praise for Anthropic’s interpretability work [E2] [E1] [E3].

ranking.summary: Google Gemini drew clearly negative sentiment because commenters criticized its data-training opt-out policy as uniquely restrictive among major providers, based on limited evidence [E2].
OpenAI came across as mixed-to-neutral: one comment criticized it for chasing enterprise and coding revenue instead of research problems, but the evidence base is limited [E3].

Anthropic was split between strong praise for interpretability and safety work and criticism that it also focuses on enterprise and coding revenue, leaving the overall tone mixed on limited evidence [E1] [E3].

evidence.excerpt: Anthropic Research going from strength to strength in interpretability. Publicly releasing the code so other labs can benefit from it is also a great move - very values aligned, and improves the overall AI safety ecosystem.

annotations.rationale: Directly praises Anthropic's interpretability work and public release as a strong, values-aligned contribution to AI safety.

evidence.excerpt: Gemini is also the only major provider where you can't opt out of using your data for training without disabling chat history.

annotations.rationale: Explicitly criticizes Gemini's data-training opt-out policy as uniquely bad among major providers.

evidence.excerpt: The AI CEOs love to pontificate about AI curing cancer, but it seems like DeepMind is the only one actively working on these research problems, while OpenAI/Anthropic largely chase enterprise/coding revenue.

annotations.rationale: Says OpenAI is mainly chasing enterprise/coding revenue rather than pursuing the praised research direction.
Groups Anthropic with OpenAI as largely prioritizing enterprise/coding revenue over the research work being praised.
